hypothesis: If generative models are pre-trained on large chemical databases (ChEMBL/ZINC-15), fine-tuned on a curated CTF dataset, and further optimized with reinforcement learning using expert-defined reward functions, then they will produce a higher fraction of valid, novel, and internally diverse Covalent Triazine Framework candidates that pass expert structural filters and exhibit improved Fréchet ChemNet Distance compared to models using only pre-training or only fine-tuning.
